glossary_mappings.ja_soft_rejection: Prospect is signaling polite disengagement through indirect communication. In Japanese business culture, phrases like 'that's interesting' often mask disagreement. Pushing further will damage trust. Send written summary instead.
glossary_mappings.ja_silence_processing: Silence in Japanese business conversations is valuable thinking time, not disengagement. Wait 3-4 seconds before speaking. Interrupting silence is disrespectful.
glossary_mappings.de_direct_honesty: German colleagues value direct criticism as a sign of respect and high engagement. Blunt feedback is not aggression. Respond with equally direct reasoning and address concerns head-on.
glossary_mappings.de_structured_agenda: German business culture prioritizes structured, time-boxed discussions with clear agendas in advance. Deviation from the plan is seen as disrespectful of everyone's time.
glossary_mappings.de_evidence_based: German teams demand concrete data and evidence before making decisions. Technical proof matters more than intuition. Come prepared with case studies and test results.
glossary_mappings.pt_high_context: Brazilian candidates communicate through stories and personal context. Core competencies are embedded in narratives, not stated directly. Listen to the full story to understand their actual skills.
glossary_mappings.pt_warmth: Brazilian culture values authentic personal expression in professional settings. Emotional investment and passion are signs of genuine engagement, not unprofessionalism.
glossary_mappings.pt_relationship_focus: For Brazilian organizations, team relationships and workplace culture are as important as individual performance. Success is collaborative and measured by team harmony.

cultural_framework_references.high_context: High-context communication relies on implied meaning, context, and what's left unsaid. Common in Asia (Japan), parts of Latin America (Brazil), and the Middle East.
cultural_framework_references.direct_communication: Direct communication values explicit, clear statements. Common in Germanic cultures (Germany), Scandinavia, and North America.
cultural_framework_references.harmony_preservation: Some cultures (Japan, to some extent Brazil) prioritize group harmony and avoid direct confrontation. Indirect communication preserves relationships.
cultural_framework_references.individualism_vs_collectivism: Individualistic cultures (US, Germany) emphasize personal achievement and clear role definition. Collectivist cultures (Japan) emphasize group consensus and harmony.
cultural_framework_references.power_distance: Some cultures have greater respect for hierarchy and authority (Japan, Brazil), while others flatten hierarchies (Germany, Scandinavia).
